task.objective: Modify the ExecutionSelector component to use the new server-side search API instead of only filtering the pre-loaded 50 executions client-side. Add debounced search input that fetches results from `/api/executions/search` and shows "Load more" functionality.

context.project_type: E2E Test Dashboard - Compare Page Enhancement
context.background:
The current ExecutionSelector component has these limitations: 1. Filters only from 50 pre-fetched executions passed as props 2. Shows maximum 5 executions per branch (hardcoded slice) 3. No indication of total execution count per branch 4. Search happens entirely client-side, cannot find older executions
Users have reported that "search is not actually working" because they cannot find commits that aren't in the top 50 most recent executions.
context.audience: - Dashboard users comparing test runs - Developers looking for specific commits or branches
context.impact: HIGH - Makes the search functionality actually useful by searching the full database

functional.requirement: Cancel pending requests when search term changes (AbortController)
Show server-side search results merged with local results
Add visual loading indicator while searching
Show total execution count per branch in header
Handle API errors gracefully with error state
Add "Load more" button per branch to fetch additional executions
Add proper ARIA attributes for accessibility
Highlight matching text in search results

non_functional.requirement: Debounce search input to avoid excessive API calls
Show results within 500ms of user stopping typing
Maintain same visual design and glass-card aesthetic

solution.approach: 1. Add new state for searchResults, isSearching, searchError 2. Create useEffect with debounced search and AbortController for cleanup 3. Use useMemo for expensive grouping/filtering operations 4. Merge searchResults with local executions (deduplicate by id) 5. Update rendering to show loading state, error state, and "Load more" buttons 6. Keep local filtering as fallback for instant feedback 7. Add ARIA attributes for accessibility

change.description: Add useExecutionSearch custom hook with AbortController
Add searchResults, isSearching, searchError state via hook
Add expandedBranches state for Load More functionality
Add searchInputRef for auto-focus on open
Add loading indicator in search input
Add execution count badge per branch
Add 'Load more' button per branch with toggle expand logic
Replace hardcoded slice(0, 5) with getVisibleExecutions function
Add ARIA attributes: role, aria-label, aria-expanded, aria-selected

steps.step: 3. Type "main" in search box - verify loading spinner appears briefly
4. Verify results include executions matching "main" branch
5. Type a commit SHA like "c60e951" - verify matching commits appear
6. Type single character "a" - verify "Type at least 2 characters" message
7. Verify "X runs" count appears next to each branch name
2. Find a branch with more than 5 executions
5. Click "Show less" to collapse back to 5
3. Verify the "main" request is cancelled (red/cancelled status)
4. Only the final "test" request should complete
3. Verify error message appears: "Search failed. Please try again."
4. Re-enable network, search again, verify it works
4. Navigate with arrow keys through results

always.rule: Show instant client-side filter results for responsive UX
Merge server results with local results (don't replace completely)
Debounce API calls to prevent excessive requests
Cancel pending requests when search term changes
Handle errors gracefully with user-friendly message

never.rule: Never make API call with less than 2 characters
Never block UI while waiting for search results
Never lose the selected execution when search returns no results
Never leave pending requests when component unmounts

output_format.structure: Modify `components/dashboard/execution-selector.tsx` with the enhanced search logic. The component maintains backwards compatibility with the same props interface.
output_format.validation: After implementation: 1. Verify component renders without errors 2. Test search with various queries 3. Confirm debouncing works (network tab shows delayed requests) 4. Confirm request cancellation works (cancelled requests in network tab) 5. Verify "Load more" expands to show all executions 6. Test accessibility with screen reader or keyboard
